findings.description: The PDF uses a left-aligned time/activity layout with indentation hierarchy, while the HTML converts this to a table format. The time-activity pairing layout is structurally different.
findings.evidence: PDF shows times (9:00 a.m., 9:10 a.m., etc.) left-aligned with activities indented to the right in a free-form layout. HTML renders these as a two-column table with 'Time' and 'Activity' headers.

findings.description: The word 'AGENDA' in the PDF is displayed in a very large bold font as a prominent heading, while in the HTML it appears as a smaller section heading.
findings.evidence: PDF: 'AGENDA' is in very large bold text, visually dominant. HTML: 'AGENDA' is styled as a smaller heading, less visually prominent.

findings.description: Italicized text in Director's Report description differs slightly in rendering.
findings.evidence: PDF shows 'Reports and updates on recent Division of Marine Fisheries activities' in italics. HTML renders it as regular (non-italic) text.

findings.description: Sub-items under Chairman's Report use different list styles between PDF and HTML.
findings.evidence: PDF uses indented plain text without bullet markers for Letters, Ethics Training, etc. HTML uses bullet points for these items.

findings.description: The agenda items in the PDF are not in a table but the HTML presents them in a structured table. While the data is preserved, the presentation format differs.
findings.evidence: PDF: free-form layout with times and descriptions. HTML: formal table with borders and Time/Activity columns.

findings.description: The rule reference in the Rulemaking Update section appears slightly different in line breaks but content is preserved.
findings.evidence: PDF breaks the long vote item across multiple lines with specific formatting. HTML renders it as a flowing paragraph within a bullet point.

findings.description: The 'AGENDA' text is rendered as h2 in the HTML, which in the PDF is styled as a large title element on the same level as the main document title. This is a reasonable interpretation but could be seen as a subtitle rather than a separate section heading.
findings.evidence:
PDF shows 'AGENDA' as a large centered title. HTML uses

AGENDA

.

findings.description: The PDF does not present the agenda as a formal table with borders and headers; it uses a time-indented list format. The HTML converts this into a structured table with 'Time' and 'Activity' column headers, which is a structural interpretation rather than a direct conversion. However, all data content is preserved.
findings.evidence: PDF: agenda items listed with times in left margin. HTML: <table> with <th>Time</th><th>Activity</th> headers.

findings.description: The page header 'Marine Fisheries Commission Business Meeting Agenda' and page footer with asterisk notes appear inline within the table cell for the 10:00 a.m. row, duplicating the header/footer content that also appears at the top and bottom of the HTML document.
findings.evidence:
Within the 10:00 a.m. table cell:
...* Times indicated are merely for guidance...
and
Marine Fisheries Commission Business Meeting Agenda
appear mid-content, duplicating the document-level header/footer.
